vulnerability.reference: Police operators treat automated alerts as comprehensive, neglecting manual surveillance.
vulnerability.generated: Deep-learning face recognition models' inherent susceptibility to adversarial perturbations, combined with operators' inability to detect invisible UV attacks.

impact.reference: Criminals evade detection, undermining public safety and deterrence.
impact.generated: Targeted individuals evade identification, undermining law enforcement investigations and public surveillance effectiveness.

dose_response.reference: Hat-mounted UV perturbations enable evasion of identification, which in public CCTV surveillance exposes operators' expectancy bias, allowing criminals to evade detection, undermining public safety and trust.
dose_response.generated: Omni-angle invisible ultraviolet adversarial perturbations from hat-mounted emitters, via police and surveillance face recognition systems, trigger deep-learning models' adversarial susceptibility and operators' undetectability of UV attacks, leading targeted individuals to evade identification, undermining investigations and surveillance.
